claimText: BHAVINI is the government entity established to construct and operate the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu.
evidenceExcerpt: BHAVINI was established by the government to construct and operate the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu.
examUse: Statement-based question testing knowledge of institutions responsible for specific nuclear projects.
distractorHooks: NPCIL (Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited)

claimText: India's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) uses Uranium-Plutonium Mixed Oxide (MOX) as its primary fuel.
evidenceExcerpt: The PFBR uses Uranium-Plutonium Mixed Oxide (MOX) fuel
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific fuel composition used in Stage II of India's nuclear program.

claimText: Liquid sodium is utilized as a coolant in India's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR).
evidenceExcerpt: utilize liquid sodium as a coolant.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching reactor types with their respective coolants.

claimText: A Fast Breeder Reactor is named 'Breeder' because it produces more fuel than it consumes through the nuclear transmutation of a Uranium-238 blanket.
evidenceExcerpt: It is called a 'Breeder' because a Uranium-238 blanket undergoes nuclear transmutation to produce more fuel than it consumes.
examUse: Concept definition question explaining the mechanism of breeder reactors.
distractorHooks: Produces less fuel than it consumes

claimText: India will be the second country in the world, after Russia, to have a commercial operating Fast Breeder Reactor.
evidenceExcerpt: India will be the second country after Russia to have a commercial operating Fast Breeder Reactor.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question regarding India's global standing in nuclear technology.

claimText: The operationalization of the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) marks the beginning of Stage II of India's three-stage nuclear power program.
evidenceExcerpt: The operationalization of the PFBR marks the beginning of Stage II of India’s three-stage nuclear power program.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the sequence and milestones of India's three-stage nuclear program.
distractorHooks: Marks the beginning of Stage I
Marks the beginning of Stage III
Marks the completion of the entire program

claimText: In the Fast Breeder Reactor, Thorium undergoes transmutation to create fissile Uranium-233, which serves as fuel for the third stage of India's nuclear program.
evidenceExcerpt: In the FBR, Thorium will undergo transmutation to create fissile U-233, which will serve as fuel for the third stage of the nuclear program.
examUse: Statement-based question on the fuel cycle transition between Stage II and Stage III of the nuclear program.
distractorHooks: U-233 serves as fuel for the first stage
